design_philosophy.core_loop: Perform aerial stunts (jumps and rotations) at precise moments during track navigation to accelerate and maintain speed while avoiding wipeouts
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between stunt execution speed and landing stability; collision with obstacles or failed landings cause accumulated speed loss
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a riderless unicycle through impossible acrobatic sequences on surreal 2D tracks
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising commitment to stunt-based progression as the sole acceleration mechanic; refuses traditional speed mechanics. Tracks use visual bar patterns (colored bars) as a coherent design language to communicate hazards and shortcuts. The game favors mechanical skill and split-second decision-making over narrative or character appeal.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players must defeat computer opponents at bronze, silver, and gold ranks progressively to advance tours. Final tour features Anti-Uni opponent with destabilizing mechanics (invisible track, reversed controls, desynchronized backgrounds) that escalate difficulty dramatically.

progression.upgrade_system: 16 different colored unicycles available with individual save files and customizable names; unicycle selection likely tied to cosmetic differentiation rather than stat changes
progression.unlock_mechanism: Nine sequential tours of five tracks each (45 total tracks). First eight tours require defeating opponents at bronze/silver/gold ranks. Final tour unlocks Anti-Uni encounter. Track progression gates further advancement.
replayability_hooks: Split-screen two-player league mode supporting up to eight players in tournament structure
Multiple difficulty ranks (bronze, silver, gold) per track encourage replay for higher ranks
16 separate save files per unicycle create identity/collection incentive
Stunt-focused gameplay rewards mastery and optimization of performance on repeated tracks

world_building_techniques: Visual track bar language: solid yellow bars indicate shortcuts; orange/yellow bars signal hazards
Environmental obstacles define track identity: speed boosts, corkscrews, loops, twists, jumps create distinctive spatial signatures
Anti-Uni final opponent introduces anomalous/surreal challenge that breaks normal track rules (invisible tracks, control reversal)

boss_design.philosophy: Anti-Uni serves as a 'boss' encounter in the final tour, representing a destabilization challenge rather than a traditional boss fight. Victory requires managing both racing mechanics and environmental chaos introduced by the opponent.
notable_bosses: Anti-Uni: Final tour opponent that causes track invisibility, control reversal, and background desynchronization upon collision—a rule-breaking antagonist that represents peak difficulty escalation

danger_signals: Anti-Uni represents a corrupting/destabilizing force in the final tour through anomalous mechanical effects
Collision mechanics create vulnerability states (speed loss, wipeout) that expose player weakness
environmental_storytelling: Track bar color coding communicates hazard proximity and shortcut locations as a universal design language
Unicycle-only aesthetic reinforces the absurdist, surreal nature of the world
Anti-Uni's effect of rendering tracks invisible suggests a reality-breaking entity opposing the player
